differentials.name: Intestinal ganglioneuromatosis = mucosal neuroma = oral + intestinal neuroganglioneuromatosis ◊  Mucosal neuromas are PATHOGNOMONIC ◊  Usually precede the appearance of thyroid carcinoma + pheochromocytoma!
differentials.raw: Intestinal ganglioneuromatosis = mucosal neuroma = oral + intestinal neuroganglioneuromatosis ◊  Mucosal neuromas are PATHOGNOMONIC ◊  Usually precede the appearance of thyroid carcinoma + pheochromocytoma!

differentials.name: nodular deformity of tongue
differentials.raw: nodular deformity of tongue (mucosal neuromas of tongue often initially diagnosed by dentists)

differentials.name: constipation alternating with diarrhea @  GI tract
differentials.raw: constipation alternating with diarrhea @  GI tract

context: =  MUCOSAL NEUROMA SYNDROME = MEN TYPE 2B Cause:  genetic defect on chromosome 10 Organ involvement (MPheG): √  thickened / plaquelike colonic wall √  chronic megacolon = dilated colon with abnormal haustral markings √  alternating areas of colonic spasm + dilatation (rarely associated with Hirschsprüng disease) √  multiple submucosal neuromas throughout small bowel, may act as lead point for intussusception
